programmes.show_description: Alf decides to put his house on the market and move into the Reads' as their lodger, but falls foul of Min's amorous advances. S7 E1: Moving in with Min
With Terry Wogan, Michael Aspel and Hot Chocolate. The legendary BAFTA-winning comedy show from Thames TV, all in the best possible taste!
Simon is asked to help a developer's daughter when a new textile formula is created and she receives a threatening letter. S3 E6: The Saint Steps In

By December 1941, did Britain know of the Pearl Harbour plans, and did Churchill warn Roosevelt so he could move the US carriers. Narrated by Robert Powell.
The USA has been at the forefront of fighting acts of terror across the world. We look at the development of the Delta force and the use of Special Units in Iran, Grenada, Panama and the Gulf War.
It was a battle no-one really wanted and a victory far greater than anyone expected - Israel fought the Six Day War and were outnumbered and outgunned. S2 E13: Shadows of the Six Day War
An exploration of London's underground railway and skyline, the origins of Metro-land - London's vast suburbia - and the clever marketing that tempted millions to move outside the city. Ep4
After the war, RAF transports helped keep Berliners alive during the Soviet blockade of the city in 1948/49 as well as flying round-the-clock operations into its airport. Ep6: Supplies From the Skies
We take a look at the Australian and New Zealand Army Corps (Anzacs) who first saw active service in Libya with the British when they forced the Italian army to surrender. Ep13: The Anzacs
Tom Selleck chronicles the personal stories of veterans and citizens who witnessed the 1941 surprise attack by the Japanese on the American Pacific Fleet launching the United States into World War II
John F. Kennedy was assassinated in Dallas, Texas, in an open-top convertible. We explore the fatal shooting of the United States' 35th president.
Tank development was dramatic in World War II; we see the emergence of monster tanks from pre-war light tanks, but were the German tanks over-engineered and too vulnerable to breakdown? Ep1: Tanks
...Were You? There are some news events that change the world - we take you back to some of Northern Ireland's darkest days and the journey towards lasting peace that was agreed on Good Friday 1998.
The brave men and women who fought along the tides of the Pacific share their stories of Japanese and US forces facing off with an intense determination and will to survive. Ep8: Hell In The Pacific
The German Army was first met with success on the Eastern Front as they took swathes of land and countless prisoners of war - later, however, their progress stalled in the icy conditions. E2
They are the means by which to confuse an enemy - from inflatable decoy tanks to fake radio broadcasts, deception played a key role in the course of WWII. S1 E9: Tools of Deception
Having brothers visit them in London causes both Richard and Hudson complications and strained relations, but will the repercussions be worse for Hudson? S2 E7: Your Obedient Servant
When his wife goes missing, Arthur is under suspicion, but he's got a more important cash flow matter to contend with. S8 E3: Whatever Happened to Her Indoors?
Exploring the world of showbiz.
What looks like a simple drugs drop at a building site turns into a murder case, and Alex is haunted by thoughts of her parents' death. S2 E7
Life in the Reads' household is very different for Alf - especially when, as he gets back from work, Min is in no rush to make his dinner. S7 E2: Min the Housekeeper
With Billy Connolly and Cliff Richard. The legendary BAFTA-winning comedy show from Thames TV, all in the best possible taste!
Simon finds himself in the Australian outback helping a father get his money back from his two greedy and argumentative sons. S3 E7: The Loving Brothers

Rudolf Hess flew to Scotland on a secret mission. Decades later he died in prison. Was it suicide or murder. Narrated by Robert Powell.
We explore America's fight against terrorism, looking at 21st Century warfare with the special forces' actions during the Battle of the Black Sea, 9/11, and the hunt for Osama Bin Laden.

We uncover Allied secrets of WWII like a battleship made of ice, bat bombs and pigeon-guided missiles; bizarre stories of extraordinary armaments dreamt up by the most inventive minds. Ep1: The Allies
First displayed in WWI, the tank is one of the most intimidating vehicles ever invented - we study the emergence of these fighting giants during WWII in northern France. S1 E1: Dawn of the Titans
We bring the RAF story into the modern era - another aircraft to have extended the RAF's capabilities is the C130 Hercules. Ep7: The Sky's The Limit

programmes.show_name: The Nazis: A Warning From History
programmes.show_description: Documentary series examining how Hitler came to power. By January 1933, Germany had voted for a chancellor who was openly opposed to democracy. Ep1: Helped Into Power
A dramatised documentary showing the disaster of the Titanic from a unique point of view.
From attacks on Pearl Harbor to the British Mediterranean Fleet and the Tirpitz, midget submarines played their part in some of the most gripping stories of the war. Ep2: Midget Submarines
There are some news events that change the world. In this episode, we look at the events of September 11th 2001.
The war reaches a critical turning point as Hitler expands it to a second front where German soldiers endure the unrelenting climate of Russia. Ep9: Eastern Front
The Messerschmitt Bf 109 was matched only by the Spitfire - it was the Luftwaffe's only single-seat fighter for the first few years of WWII and the RAF's nemesis during the Battle of Britain.
The 'Desert Fox' Field Marshal Rommel seemed to have a sixth sense when it came to Allied plans and positions - we take a look into how Rommel's winning streak was broken. S1 E10: Rommel's Enigma
Elizabeth and her daughter return to Eaton Place, but Sarah dislikes Nanny Webster as she is very set in her ways and seems not to be able to cope as she used to. S2 E8: Out of the Everywhere
Arthur keeps acquiring items that won't do his reputation any good - how can he offload them without the rest of the manor finding out he's been conned? S8 E4: Three Cons Make a Mountain
Rigsby is hoping to take new tenant Brenda out for the evening, but he has a cashflow problem and a string of unwelcome visitors who start arriving at the house. S2 E6: Last of the Big Spenders
Mr. Brown becomes ill and cannot come to class. The students all pay him a visit, each with their own 'remedy' to make him feel better. S1 E9: Kill or Cure
Alex has caught a hospital infection and the doctors in 2008 are losing hope for her - a wedge develops between her and Gene and it threatens to tear the team apart. S2 E8
